mentions.note: Best-of-breed company under CEO Larry Culp; stock ran from $62 to $318 in two years; reported fantastic Q4 (beats on every line, remarkable margin improvement) yet stock dropped 7.4%; lesson: A-student getting more A's doesn't get praise if traders expect A+; too-loved going in; momentum players disappointed by lack of upside surprise; do not expect more rallies here.
Maintenance-heavy business insulated from TSA/fuel pressures; CEO Larry Culp solid; Boeing preferred but GEA also a buy.
Reports Tuesday; may disappoint due to aircraft maintenance downtime during war; watch conference call for forward guidance.

Fantastic business hitting all-time high today; Larry Culp executing well. Caller who started accumulating in late $200s showed courage sticking through downturn; well-positioned for continued appreciation.
